title: Thermal Spray Coating Vision QC Inspector
description: Automated coating quality inspector that indexes a coated sample on a stepper turntable, fires an 850 nm IR strobe, captures surface images with an ESP32-CAM, streams them to a Raspberry Pi for histogram-based porosity analysis, and publishes pass/fail results via MQTT with fault logging on threshold breach.
objective: Inspect full 360° coating circumference; compute porosity at each 10° step; flag coating if porosity > 2% at any sector; publish MQTT JSON report; log all results.
constraints: Arduino Uno + ESP32-CAM + Raspberry Pi Zero W; stepper turntable; IR LED strobe; WiFi LAN; BOM < $80.
supporting_fact_titles: Thermal Spray WC-Co Coating Porosity and Adhesion Strength Correlation
design_element_titles: Arduino 3D Scanner — Stepper Turntable + TOF Sensor
Raspberry Pi Zero W Edge Vision Processor
element_usage_notes: Stepper turntable indexes sample at 10° steps; TOF sensor confirms sample presence before each capture
ESP32-CAM captures 320×240 JPEG under IR strobe illumination; serves image via HTTP on port 81
IR strobe fired 1 ms before shutter; provides oblique illumination for delamination contrast
RPi Zero W fetches image, runs coating_porosity_histogram, publishes JSON to MQTT broker

element_interactions.from: Arduino 3D Scanner — Stepper Turntable + TOF Sensor
element_interactions.description: Turntable step-complete signal fires IR strobe 50 ms after motor settles
1 ms IR strobe illuminates surface; ESP32-CAM shutter triggered simultaneously

element_interactions.to: Raspberry Pi Zero W Edge Vision Processor
element_interactions.description: JPEG streamed to RPi via HTTP GET for OpenCV porosity analysis

element_interactions.from: Raspberry Pi Zero W Edge Vision Processor
element_interactions.to: Arduino 3D Scanner — Stepper Turntable + TOF Sensor
element_interactions.description: RPi sends HTTP ACK to Arduino after analysis; Arduino advances turntable to next step
